frameworks.name: theory of mind
frameworks.one_line: mental-state attribution observers use to predict others' actions

frameworks.name: model-based vs habitual control
frameworks.one_line: dual-system distinction between planned and reinforcement-driven behavior

frameworks.name: rational planning
frameworks.one_line: treats others as goal-directed agents executing efficient plans

core_question: Does human theory of mind include a representation of habitual (non-planned) action, and when is it deployed?
key_assumptions: observers infer others' behavior by attributing mental states
current rational-actor accounts treat others as efficient planners given knowledge and goals
behavior can arise from either planning or reinforcement-driven habit
manipulations: factors that shift the balance between habitual and planned behavior in the observed agent
key_findings: human theory of mind is sensitive to factors influencing whether an actor behaves habitually vs by planning
observers do not exclusively interpret others' behavior as rational planning
interpretations: theory of mind incorporates a theory of habitual action alongside rational planning
purely rational-actor accounts of mental-state attribution are incomplete
